description: P2-A: figure extraction recall uplift benchmark. Tests whether enabling ENABLE_FIGURE_ANALYSIS causes the pipeline to extract gene symbols that appear as labels in figures (heatmaps, volcano plots, oncoprints) but may be missed from prose text alone.
oa_check_method: PMC OA API (pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/tools/idconv) + confirmed FTP package availability

papers.title: Comprehensive molecular portraits of human breast tumours
papers.figure_description: Figure 1 is an SMG oncoprint with gene symbols as labeled rows (TP53, PIK3CA, GATA3, MAP3K1, CDH1, PTEN, EGFR, ERBB2 etc.). Figure 2 is a multi-platform expression heatmap grouped by subtype. Figure 4 shows MEMo mutual exclusivity modules with named gene nodes.
papers.oa_license: none (PMC OA subset, NIH-funded TCGA; FTP package available)
papers.notes: TCGA Breast Cancer 2012. SMG oncoprint (Figure 1) has gene names as row labels — ideal for figure extraction testing. All 6 union genes already extracted from text; CDH1 and PTEN may be uplift candidates if only visible in figure labels.

papers.title: Integrated genomic analyses of ovarian carcinoma
papers.figure_description: Figure 2 is a gene expression clustering heatmap separating tumor samples into four molecular subtypes with labeled gene tracks. Figure 3 shows pathway alteration diagrams with gene-level annotations for BRCA1, BRCA2, NF1, RB1, CDK12, CCNE1.
papers.oa_license: none (PMC OA subset, NIH-funded TCGA; FTP package available)
papers.notes: TCGA Ovarian Cancer 2011. Expression heatmap (Figure 2) shows gene-labeled tracks across subtype clusters. FOXM1 is labeled in the pathway figure (Figure 3) and may be a figure-only discovery relative to prose extraction. Pipeline already finds all 8 genes including FOXM1 — figure analysis should reinforce these.

papers.title: Mutational landscape and significance across 12 major cancer types
papers.figure_description: Figure 2 is the central figure: a large heatmap/oncoprint of 127 SMGs with gene names as row labels and cancer type columns showing mutation frequency percentages. Figure 4 is a mutation-status clustering heatmap with labeled gene rows. Extended Data figures include additional SMG oncoprints.
papers.oa_license: CC BY-NC-SA (PMC3927368; FTP + PDF packages available)
papers.notes: Kandoth et al 2013 Pan-Cancer. The most figure-rich candidate: Figure 2 lists all 127 SMGs with gene symbols as row labels across 12 cancer types — the canonical example where figure extraction matters. WARNING: high Jaccard variance (min=0.017, mean=0.345) across 3 runs, suggesting run 3 was truncated or degraded. Ideal for testing whether figure analysis stabilises extraction of secondary SMGs visible only as row labels.

papers.title: Imbalanced Host Response to SARS-CoV-2 Drives Development of COVID-19
papers.figure_description: Figure 1 contains a heatmap of DEGs with labeled gene rows (ISG15, MX1, IFIT1, IL6, CXCL8 etc.). Figure 3 includes a heatmap of ferret DEGs. Figure 4 shows volcano plots with individual gene symbols labeled as dots (ACE2, IL6, CXCL8, CXCL9 visible as named outlier points). This is the only candidate with volcano plots — key for type diversity.
papers.oa_license: none (PMC OA subset, emergency COVID OA mandate; FTP package available)
papers.notes: Blanco-Melo et al 2020 Cell. Provides both heatmap and volcano plot figure types — essential for benchmark type diversity. Current text-only pipeline extracts only IL6 (1 of 9 expected genes), making this the paper with the highest potential figure-extraction uplift. Chemokines (CXCL8, CXCL9, CXCL16, CCL2, CCL8) and IFN-response genes (MX1, STAT1) appear as labeled points in volcano plots and labeled rows in heatmaps.

skipped_candidates.title: Integrated genomic analysis identifies clinically relevant subtypes of glioblastoma characterized by abnormalities in PDGFRA, IDH1, EGFR, and NF1
skipped_candidates.reason_skipped: Two issues: (1) PMC OA API explicitly returns 'idIsNotOpenAccess' error for PMC2818769 — it is an NIH Author Manuscript (NIHMS prefix) accessible via PMC efetch but not in the formal OA subset. (2) Figure content is less suitable: Figure 2 is an 840-gene expression heatmap where individual gene symbols are not legibly labeled in the image, and Figure 3 is an integrated alteration heatmap. The key 4 genes (EGFR, NF1, PDGFRA, IDH1) are prominently named in the title and abstract, so figure extraction provides minimal uplift over text extraction for this paper.

acceptance_criteria.description: P2-A measures whether ENABLE_FIGURE_ANALYSIS increases recall of genes that appear in figures but are missed by text-only extraction
acceptance_criteria.target_uplift: > 0 on at least 2 of the 4 papers
acceptance_criteria.secondary_metric: no precision loss: figure mode must not introduce more false positives than recall gains
acceptance_criteria.focus_paper: 32416070 (COVID) — highest uplift potential, only 1/9 expected genes currently extracted
